taxonomy: Algernonia amazonica is a member of the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family, which comprises about 2,800 species of flowering plants. This family is one of the largest in the angiosperm clade. The genus Algernonia is part of the subfamily Acalyphoideae, which is a subfamily of the Euphorbiaceae family. The classification of Algernonia amazonica is further divided into the tribe Acalypheae, within which it is a part of the subtribe Acalyphinae. The exact classification of Algernonia amazonica may vary depending on the source and the current understanding of plant taxonomy.
uses: Algernonia amazonica is reported to have various medicinal properties. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Its extracts have also been studied for potential antiviral and antioxidant activities. However, further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for medical use.
